title: GDPO: Group reward-Decoupled Normalization Policy Optimization for Multi-reward RL Optimization

summary.problem: Directly applying GRPO normalization to multi-reward RL settings causes distinct reward combinations to collapse into identical advantage values, reducing training signal resolution.
summary.method: GDPO performs per-reward decoupled normalization (zero-mean, unit-variance for each reward independently), then sums individual normalized advantages, and applies batch-wise normalization. Introduces conditioned rewards for handling objectives with large difficulty gaps.
summary.innovation: Identifies the 'advantage collapse' problem in multi-reward GRPO — N binary rewards map to only 2 unique advantage groups instead of 2^N. Proposes decoupled normalization and conditioned rewards (gate easier objectives on harder ones being satisfied first).
summary.results: Improvements across 3 domains: Tool calling (BFCL-v3: +2.6%), Math reasoning (AIME: +6.3% at 1.5B with 40% fewer length violations), Coding (CodeContests: +2.4%). Works across Qwen2.5 and DeepSeek-R1 families at 1.5B-7B scale.
summary.one_liner: GDPO 通过对每个 reward 独立归一化再聚合，解决了多奖励 GRPO 训练中 advantage 坍缩为相同值的问题，在三个任务上一致优于 GRPO。
